methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $117M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
VEIR is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $117M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates two methods across a peer set of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables (KoBold Metals at $3.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M), alongside reference to a broader 9-company comp universe including public peer LNZA and private leaders such as Helion and Redwood Materials.

The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $117M total raised proxy, implying a $222M valuation. The peer-median method references the 3-company median valuation of $1.0B, with the peer range spanning $700M to $3.0B. These two approaches yield a triangulated range of $189M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $611M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty: stage-progression anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while peer-median captures significant valuation heterogeneity within the Climate & Energy sector at the Series C stage.

Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x step-up multiplier for this specific market and technology vertical, and whether the Series C peer set fully reflects VEIR's risk profile or market position within the broader frontier-tech landscape. The $611M median represents a reasonable central estimate pending closer examination of unit economics and technical de-risking relative to named comparables.
